title: Ibanjji

'''Ibanjji''' was a terrestrial<ref name="DES" /> [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="CSWE" /> located in the [[Ibanjji system/Legends|Ibanjji system]], a part of the [[Mieru'kar sector/Legends|Mieru'kar sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of<ref name="TEA Ibanjji" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The conditions on Ibanjji were harsh, and it was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to packs of creatures known as [[Varn (creature)|varns]]. During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], it was the site of a special [[Ibanjji motivational camp|motivational camp]]<ref name="DES" /> used to harden Imperial recruits.<ref name="CSWE" />

On the recommendation of his Imperial [[Sub-Adult Group/Legends|Sub-Adult Group]] neighborhood warden, [[Titus Klev]], a thirteen-[[Standard year/Legends|year]]-old SAGroup member from the<ref name="DES" /> [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> planet [[Alsakan/Legends|Alsakan]], was selected to attend the motivational camp on Ibanjji<ref name="DES" /> as part of his training<ref name="CSWE" /> at some point before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]<ref name="DES" /> of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].<ref name="TEA" /> During his [[Time/Legends|time]] at the motivational camp, Klev saved an [[Instructor/Legends|instructor]] from a wild pack of varns. As a result, the recruit received approval to join Imperial [[CompForce/Legends|CompForce]] despite being four years under the standard age.<ref name="DES" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ibanjji was first mentioned in the [[1993]] ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'', which was authored by [[Michael Allen Horne]] and published by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="DES" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Ibanjji [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the world Ibanjji, in grid square N-4.<ref name="TEA Ibanjji" />
